name: Stewed Hijiki
recipeIngredient: About 2/3 of 2.1 oz bag of dried hijiki seaweed
A few green beans (french or slivered)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the hijiki in water to cover for 15 minutes.
2. Soak the mushrooms in warm water until soft, then thinly slice them and keep the soaking water.
3. Drain the hijiki (cut it into smaller pieces if needed). Simmer the hijiki with the mushrooms and their soaking water in enough water to cover for about 20 minutes, or until softened.
4. Cut carrot into matchsticks, green beans into french slivers, and konnyaku into small cubes if using.
5. Add the vegetables, soy sauce, and sugar to the simmering mixture. Adjust seasoning by adding more soy sauce if desired (avoid salt as it can make the hijiki tough).
6. Simmer covered until the carrot is soft.
7. Serve slightly warm or at room temperature; this dish also tastes good cold.
